questions.text: What does the Credit Limit define in terms of customer transactions?
options: The minimum amount a customer must spend annually
The numerical limit on the maximum credit exposure a customer can have at any given time
The number of transactions a customer can make in a month
The credit score of the customer

questions.text: Which interface is used for Insurance/Credit Limit in Credit Limit Management?
What happens when an order exceeds the credit limit in SAP?
options: A credit increase is automatically applied
The order is processed without checks
The order is deleted from the system

questions.text: Which of the following is NOT used in calculating a customer's credit score?
What is the purpose of a dynamic credit limit check?
options: To block credit based on past sales data
To ignore the credit limit entirely
To update the customer's credit score instantly
To consider the order’s requested delivery date in the credit check

questions.text: Credit Limit Management includes a Credit Limit Request Workflow internal to SAP.
External Credit Agency interfaces are not utilized for credit rating in Credit Management.
Static and Dynamic Credit Limit Checks consider the same variables for blocking credit.
The Risk Class is a qualitative tag that is automatically updated and cannot be manually changed.
